content.assessment: This proposal requires the highest actor capability and sequencing burden of all options: a full comprehensive-school restructuring demands workforce capacity, curriculum redesign, and sustained political durability. The lens surfaces a specific implementation gap — an aging, undersized, under-resourced teaching workforce is asked to execute a reform with system-wide fidelity requirements, while comparative precedent shows reversal risk even after measured success.
opportunities: Sequencing could be phased regionally or by cohort to reduce workforce strain, though this is not specified in the mechanism as described
Could be coupled with parallel teacher-training investment to close the capacity gap identified in the workforce finding
strengths: Clear comparative precedent (Poland) demonstrates the mechanism is deliverable in principle at national scale
Addresses selection-point proliferation directly rather than symptomatically
threats: High symbolic salience and demonstrated opposition-mobilization capacity threaten sequencing durability before implementation matures
Poland's post-2016 reversal despite measured success indicates that even successful delivery does not guarantee containment against future political reversal, undermining long-horizon planning value
weaknesses: Teacher workforce capacity (aging profile, low pay, low replacement rate) is a documented constraint on implementation fidelity for a reform requiring widespread buy-in (F-live-implementation_planning-07)
Governance centralization has produced bottlenecks rather than implementation-ready central capacity, complicating top-down sequencing of a major structural reform (F-live-implementation_planning-03)
